A partial reflection in the newest photo captured a sliver of the Riverside Overlook's rusted gantry crane, visible from only one vantage point above the water.
Mr. Han was present, unlogged, in the Storage Facility's visitor sign-in gap on at least three of the nights units were accessed.

knows: Han has confessed to engineering the drive, the GPS trail, the break-in, and the entire game as an audition
hides: Hasn't told anyone — not Lina, not Yuki, not Marcus — that some part of him understood, for one terrible second, why Han thought he'd say yes
believes_falsely: Believes turning Han over fully closes the matter, before the debt payoff detail forces him to reconsider

knows: Han all but admits familiarity with her old case's framing style but won't confirm details outright
believes_falsely: Still doesn't know for certain whether Han was responsible for her old case, and may never find out

knows: Confirmed the paper stock, the key timeline, and the GPS trail all trace back to Han alone
Confesses selectively, framing decades of killings as devotion to finding someone worthy
hides: The full number and names of every victim, and refuses to confirm Yuki's specific case — his last deliberate act of control
believes_falsely: Believes even now that Ethan might still say yes eventually, mistaking his horror for reluctance rather than refusal

case.label: The Second Byline
case.solution: Lina's carefully guarded anonymous source turns out to be the same storage-facility clerk who, unknown to her, had an informal decade-long relationship with Han as a tenant — meaning her exclusive was shaped, without her ever knowing it, by information moving in the killer's own orbit rather than a righteous whistleblower's conscience.
objective_facts: Lina's source used a burner phone purchased two blocks from Han's Camera Shop.
The source is confirmed to be the same storage-facility clerk who informally warned Marcus's contact chain about police interest.
Lina never disclosed the source's identity to her editor before the connection to Han's orbit was confirmed.

knows: The burner's purchase location ties Lina's source to the same neighborhood as Han's shop
hides: Doesn't push Lina further on it in the moment, giving her room in the wake of the larger reveal

knows: Her 'exclusive' scoop was, in part, shaped by someone connected to the very killer she was chasing
hides: Hasn't decided whether to disclose the source's compromised nature to her editor before the story runs

knows: Confirms the burner trace closes the loop back to a known associate of Han's

case.label: Trust at the Lab
case.solution: Marcus, quietly resentful of always being the second name on Ethan's case files, spent months trading inside details of the investigation to a storage-facility clerk in exchange for favors and validation; that clerk's casual warnings reached Han's own orbit, repeatedly buying the killer time to clear trophy units before the team arrived, and the same clerk turns out to be Lina's anonymous source as well — making Marcus's carelessness an unwitting but real obstruction with consequences no one has fully priced yet.
objective_facts: Storage unit contents were cleared out within 48 hours on at least two occasions when Marcus discussed active leads with his outside contact.
Marcus's contact had known Han informally as a longtime tenant for over a decade.
The same contact is confirmed to be the anonymous source who had been feeding Lina information for months.
Marcus never reported the relationship or the shared details to Ethan, Yuki, or lab supervision.

knows: Confronted with Yuki's phone records, is forced to admit the relationship and its timing to the whole team
Learns his contact was also the source feeding Lina all season
The full extent of Marcus's leak and its cost to the investigation
hides: Hasn't told Marcus whether he'll vouch for him with the department review — genuinely undecided

knows: Marcus will face formal review
The leak explains real gaps in her own case timeline
Her suspicions about Marcus were correct all along
hides: Still hasn't told him she suspected for weeks and said nothing, ashamed of her own silence

case.label: The Shop's Ledger
case.solution: The Cho Print Shop's crippling back-rent debt was quietly paid off in full by an anonymous benefactor during the crisis in episode five; the money came from Mr. Han, funneled through a cashier's check to look like a forgiving landlord or lucky break — a calculated act meant to bind Ethan and Lina closer to him with gratitude before revealing what he truly wanted from Ethan.
objective_facts: The Cho Print Shop's landlord received full back-payment via a cashier's check purchased at a bank branch two blocks from Han's Camera Shop, the same week the eviction notice was due.
No family member, insurer, or bank loan accounts for the source of the payment.

knows: The 'miracle' that saved her family's shop came from the same man who terrorized her boyfriend all season
hides: Hasn't told her parents the money came from a serial killer, unsure they could bear knowing it

knows: Han paid off the debt deliberately, as leverage and as a 'gift' meant to make Ethan complicit before he even knew the rules of the game
hides: Hasn't told Lina how much this specific detail sickens him — that even her family's relief was a chess move

knows: Exactly what he did with the payment and why
hides: Never intended the payment to be traced, but doesn't deny it once confronted
believes_falsely: Believes the gesture will be remembered as kindness despite everything else, misjudging how thoroughly it disgusts Lina and Ethan
